data.title: Alibaba rallies as cloud growth offsets China price war pressures
data.description:
Alibaba (NYSE:BABA) broke higher in early trading despite posting second quarter results below the expectations of analysts.

Revenue was up 2% year-over-year to RMB247,652 million ($34.6 billion), which was below the consensus expectation. Excluding revenue from the disposed businesses of Sun Art and Intime, revenue on a like-for-like basis would have grown by 10% year-over-year.

Customer management revenue grew 10% and revenue from Cloud Intelligence Group grew 26%, with AI-related product revenue achieving triple-digit growth for the eighth consecutive quarter. Local e-commerce growth was weak during the quarter amid intense competition and deflationary pressures in China, with rivals like PDD (PDD) and JD.com (JD) aggressively cutting prices again.

Operating income dropped 3%, and non-GAAP net income declined by 18% year-over-year, weighed down by investments in new rapid commerce and AI initiatives.

"Our decisive investment in the quick commerce business achieved key milestones as we won consumer mindshare. We generated substantial synergies from combining resources of our consumer platforms which resulted in new highs in monthly active consumers and daily order volume," stated CEO  Eddie Wu. "Driven by robust AI demand, Cloud Intelligence Group experienced accelerated revenue growth, and AI-related product revenue is now a significant portion of revenue from external customers," he added.

On Seeking Alpha, Danil Sereda, Investing Group Leader for Beyond the Wall Investing, said that once Alibaba's (NYSE:BABA) one-offs that negatively impacted sales/earnings in the past quarters are gone, the company's growth should accelerate. Sereda remained bullish on the e-commerce stock.

Shares of Alibaba (BABA) were up 3.7% in premarket trading on Friday.

data.title: Lisa Cook hints at clerical error in mortgage dispute; new criminal referral filed
data.description:
Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook's counsel has suggested that a "clerical error" may have been behind any mortgage application discrepancy over which President Donald Trump wants her fired.

"… the 'potential[ity]' that Governor Cook mislabeled a home's purpose on a mortgage application well before her Board appointment without any allegation of its intentionality or materiality would not be the type of 'offense' that would constitute 'cause'" for her removal, Cook's lawyers wrote in a court filing seeking a temporary restraining order.

"Allowing such a charge to be the basis for Presidential action with no investigation, substantiation, formal charge or court finding could hardly be enough evidence on which a 'cause' firing could occur," they added. 

The filing also noted that Trump and Bill Pulte, director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency, did not allege explicitly that Cook "benefited from any clerical error, or that such an error was intentional."

A hearing on the motion for a temporary restraining order is scheduled for Friday morning. The motion is part of Cook's lawsuit to block Trump's effort to oust her from the Fed Board of Governors.

Meanwhile, Pulte has filed a second criminal referral with the Department of Justice against Cook. This relates to a mortgage on her condominium and alleged misrepresentations of two homes she owns during her time as a Fed governor.

"3 strikes and you're out," Pulte posted on social media platform X.

data.title: SoFi CEO enters $24.1M prepaid stock deal on 1.5M shares
data.description:
SoFi Technologies (NASDAQ:SOFI) CEO Anthony Noto entered into a financial agreement called a prepaid variable forward contract with a third-party dealer involving 1.5 million SoFi shares.

These shares represent about 7% of his holdings and less than 1% of SoFi’s total outstanding shares.

Under the deal, Noto receives an upfront payment of over $24.1 million. The contract is scheduled to mature on or about August 28, 2028, at which time, Noto may deliver shares of the company's common stock or elect to settle the contract in cash, depending on SoFi’s share price at that time.

Noto pledged the 1.5M shares to secure his obligations under the contract, but he will continue to keep voting, dividends, and other rights in the pledged shares while the contract is in effect.

data.title: Anheuser-Busch's new energy drink partnership hits 7-Eleven stores
data.description:
Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD) has introduced a new energy drink called Phorm Energy into 7-Eleven stores across the United States this month. The rollout follows an earlier online-only debut. Anheuser-Busch (BUD) is making its aggressive move into the energy drink category in partnership with St. Louis-based 1st Phorm and UFC President Dana White.

Phorm Energy comes in four flavors: Blue Blitz, Orange Fury, Grape Smash, and Screamin' Freedom. Each 16-ounce can features 200mg of caffeine from green tea extract, an electrolyte blend for hydration, and contains zero sugar and no artificial flavors. The product is designed for consumers seeking both physical and mental energy and is positioned as a drink for those "who show up and do the work" every day. A promotion runs through the end of the year, offering two cans for $5 exclusively at participating 7-Eleven stores for customers aged 18 and up.

 

 

 

The keyword Phorm Energy saw a notable spike of Google search interest in June and is still trending strong.

data.title: Gabriel Resources announces C$3.6M units offering
data.description:
• Gabriel Resources (TSXV:GBU:CA) is offering up to 34,305,000 units at C$0.105 each to raise gross proceeds of ~C$3.6 million (US$2.625 million).
  • Each unit will consist of one common share in the capital of the company and one common share purchase warrant. Each warrant will entitle the holder to purchase one share for a period of five years from the date of issuance at an exercise price of C$0.14 per share.
  • The net proceeds of the offering are intended to be used for general corporate purposes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Lotus Technology reports 1H results
data.description:
• Lotus Technology press release (NASDAQ:LOT): 1H net loss was $313 million for the first half of 2025, narrowed by 32% YoY.
  • Revenue of $218M (+45% Y/Y)
  • Gross margin for the first half was 8.2%, versus 12.8% for the first half of 2024.
  • Operating loss was $263 million for the first half of 2025, narrowed by 40% YoY.
